summary_one_line: Input must carry well-formed URL/protocol literals (scheme '://', port '9001', 'HTTP/' token) so the parser advances the connect/FTP state machine to the socket-close path where data->multi is NULL.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structured input whose leading bytes form a parseable URL with a scheme separator '://' (offsets ~0x0a-0x0c) and a numeric port like '9001' (offsets ~0x19-0x1c) followed by a protocol token 'HTTP/' / 'GHTTP/27.0' near offset 0x24, driving curl's connect/FTP state machine deep enough that Curl_closesocket -> Curl_multi_closed runs while data->multi is NULL (false side of if(multi)).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds are 131-byte structured inputs containing '://', port digits '9001', and 'GHTTP/27.0' literals; their per-offset byte sets are concentrated on these protocol characters (e.g. '/'x14 at 0x0c, '9'x15 at 0x17, '0'x14 at 0x19) and they take the false branch, with winner avg hitcount 332 vs loser 13.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short 35-byte low-structure inputs dominated by '%','+','A' fill bytes with no coherent '://' / port / 'HTTP/' literals; they take the true branch and the state machine never reaches the socket-close path with multi==NULL.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads intercepted string/integer CMPs in curl's URL and protocol parser and substitutes the exact expected literals ('://', port number, 'HTTP/') into the input, letting it pass the comparison gates that advance the connect/FTP state machine; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot synthesize these multi-byte literals by chance.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of if(multi) in Curl_multi_closed.
Both decisive pairs differ only by the I2S technique.
The cmplog>naive pair is also an I2S delta.
Winner takes the false branch and hits the branch far more often than the loser.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=332  loser=13

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving seeds are 131-byte structured inputs containing a 'GHTTP/27.0' protocol literal.
Winner seeds contain the '://' scheme separator and port-like digits.
Loser-blocking seeds are short 35-byte inputs that take the true branch.
Loser seeds are 35 bytes, much shorter than the 131-byte winners.
At offset 0x0c the winner bytes concentrate on '/' while losers are diffuse non-slash bytes.
At offset 0x24 winners concentrate on 'G' (start of the HTTP token) where losers do not.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a matching CMP operand into the input at the matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator, isolating the I2S delta.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 322 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 32d10b449c4075ee` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 23 and 24 of the 25-level ancestor chain; the tool's I2S-floor signal line confirms unwrapped I2SRandReplace finds under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating if(multi==NULL) behind the URL/protocol literals where value_profile resolves the branch as fast as cmplog/vpc (no I2S advantage), showing the divergence is driven by CMP_MAP gradient rather than I2S literal substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from the protocol literals in the input to data->multi being NULL at the socket-close path is inferred from the call chain and source, not directly observed in a per-seed trace.
